metadata.original_prompt: Create a new python package called 'math_utils'. It must contain an __init__.py, a calc.py with a factorial function, and a test_calc.py that asserts factorial(5) == 120. Please generate them together in one pass.

tasks.title: Create math_utils/__init__.py
tasks.description: Generate the __init__.py file in the math_utils directory to initialize the package.

tasks.title: Create math_utils/calc.py with factorial function
tasks.description: Implement a factorial function in calc.py using iterative or recursive logic.

tasks.title: Create math_utils/test_calc.py
tasks.description: Write a test that imports factorial from calc and asserts factorial(5) == 120.

solution.approach: Analyze the task requirements: create an __init__.py file in the math_utils directory to initialize a Python package. Determine that this is a minimal initialization file with no immediate functionality required, but it must exist and be valid Python code.

Design the content of __init__.py: since the task only requires package initialization and no specific exports or logic are mentioned, the file will contain only an empty pass statement or a docstring. This satisfies Python's requirement for a valid module while keeping it minimal.
Create the math_utils/__init__.py file with the content 'pass' to initialize the package. Ensure the parent directory math_utils exists and write the file using standard file I/O operations.
solution.algorithm: Check if directory exists; if not, create it. Open __init__.py for writing and insert 'pass'. Close the file.

solution.approach: Validate that the __init__.py file was successfully created in the correct location (math_utils/__init__.py) and contains the expected content ('pass'). Confirm the file is readable and the package can be imported without errors.
solution.algorithm: Use os.path.exists() to verify file presence, read and check contents, then attempt a basic import test (e.g., 'import math_utils') and catch any exceptions.
